page: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadsv7 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 5669 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data highlights the high-performance capabilities of this instance. In CPU-bound workloads, the server achieves top-tier results in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, as well as Passmark CPU and encryption evaluations. Memory performance is similarly robust, with top-tier write bandwidth for large blocks and strong read bandwidth. These capabilities translate to exceptional throughput in data processing tasks, as demonstrated by top-tier performance in Redis SET operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial 5.6 TB local storage capacity, the D96ads_v7 offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise workloads. The absence of a GPU limits its use in heavy deep learning training, but its top-tier LLM inference benchmarks for small and medium models make it highly capable for CPU-based AI inference. The server is optimally configured for large-scale relational databases, high-throughput web serving, intensive data compression pipelines, and enterprise-grade general-purpose applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, powered by a 96-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and includes 5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks, database operations, and memory write bandwidth. While it lacks dedicated GPU hardware, its high-frequency AMD processor and massive local storage make it highly efficient for demanding workloads. The D96ads_v7 is optimally suited for large-scale database hosting, high-throughput web serving, intensive data processing, and CPU-based LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, optimized for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ads_v7 is a 96-vCPU AMD EPYC virtual machine with 384 GB RAM and 5669 GB local storage, built for demanding database and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density virtual machine combining ninety-six high-frequency AMD vCPUs with massive local storage for demanding enterprise database workloads.

Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM and 5669 GB of local storage
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
